name: Flush dirty submodule pointers bottom-up
description: Detects repos with dirty submodule pointers (` M submodule-path` in git status) and recursively commits from leaves upward using git-submodule-flush
fix_script: git-submodule-flush --execute --panic-attack --skip idaptik --message 'chore: flush submodule changes' --author 'Jonathan D.A. Jewell <6759885+hyperpolymath@users.noreply.github.com>' {{repo_path}}
fix_script_json_mode: git-submodule-flush --execute --json --panic-attack --skip idaptik --message 'chore: flush submodule changes' --author 'Jonathan D.A. Jewell <6759885+hyperpolymath@users.noreply.github.com>' {{repo_path}}
rollback: git reset HEAD~1 (per repo, bottom-up order reversed to top-down)

detection_rule.trigger: git status --porcelain shows ` M <path>` where <path>/.git exists
